purpose: Evaluation corpus for joint-overlay registration features: successful registrations, nearby offset negatives, hard harmonically-compatible negatives, and multiple mashup technique families, across song pairs. Labels are evaluation truth for leave-one-song-pair-out testing — they must NEVER be read by discovery or probe code as inputs. See docs/experiment-joint-registration-features.md.

label_taxonomy.success: a registration a human auditioned (or attested) as musically working
label_taxonomy.near_offset_negative: a small whole-bar shift of a success (e.g. -1/-2/-3 bars) presumed or judged not to work
label_taxonomy.hard_harmonic_negative: harmonically compatible by per-track analysis but judged not to work in combination
label_taxonomy.random_negative: an arbitrary registration with no reason to work

label_states.annotated: labeled from human audition or explicit user attestation (method required)
label_states.hypothesis: presumed label awaiting audition — usable to plan auditions, NEVER as evaluation truth

honesty_notes: n=1 pair as of 2026-07-11: no cross-pair claim of any kind is supported by this corpus yet.
Most near-offset negatives on the first pair are hypotheses, not auditioned negatives — auditioning them is the highest-value labeling work.

Offsets are in the anchor frame: guest bar 1 delayed N host bars past the first-stable-downbeat coincidence. Labels attach to the stated conform (grid/pitch); a different conform is a different audition.
Do not commit audio; local_path entries are machine-local and may be missing — skip, never fail.
